channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] allenai/olmocr — Toolkit for linearizing PDFs for LLM datasets/training
item.description: Allen AI's olmocr toolkit linearizes PDFs into sequential text for LLM dataset preparation.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] togatoga/karukan — Japanese Input Method System for Linux, macOS, Neural Kana-Kanji Conversion Engine
item.description: karukan is an open-source Japanese input method system with neural kana-kanji conversion for Linux and macOS.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] TencentCloud/CubeSandbox — Instant, Concurrent, Secure & Lightweight Sandbox for AI Agents.
item.description: TencentCloud's CubeSandbox is an instant, concurrent, secure, and lightweight sandbox for AI agents.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] yikart/AiToEarn — Let's use AI to Earn!
item.description: AiToEarn is a GitHub-trending project proposing AI-based income generation methods.

item.title: [Hacker News] Godot will no longer accept AI-authored code contributions
item.description: Godot will no longer accept AI-authored code contributions

item.title: [Latent Space] AIEWF Daily Dispatch: Loops, Software Factories & Forward Deployed Engineers
item.description: AIEWF Daily Dispatch: Loops, Software Factories & Forward Deployed Engineers

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Single Rewrite Suffices: Empirical Lessons from Production Skill Description Optimization
item.description: Single LLM rewrite of skill descriptions resolves overlapping-description routing failures in enterprise AI agents, matching manual tuning with 32x engineering speedup.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Indi-RomCoM: Code-Mixed Benchmark for Evaluating LLMs on Romanized Indic-English Instructions
item.description: Indi-RomCoM benchmark evaluates LLMs on Romanized Indic-English code-mixed instructions, with performance degrading as code-mixing intensity increases.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Using AI Agents to Automate Black-Box Audits of Personalization Algorithms at Scale
item.description: Researchers deployed AI agents with fixed personas to conduct counterfactual audits of platform personalization algorithms.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Calibration Rankings Reverse: Accuracy-Controlled Evaluation for Fair Comparison of LLMs
item.description: LLM calibration rankings reverse when controlling for accuracy; study proposes ACE, an accuracy-controlled evaluation framework.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When transformers learn "impossible" languages, what do they learn?
item.description: Transformers struggle with "impossible" languages due to generative production failures rather than grammatical sensitivity deficiencies, explaining non-attestation in human languages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Test-Time Verification for Text-to-SQL via Outcome Reward Models
item.description: Outcome Reward Models enable semantic-based verification for Text-to-SQL, achieving 4.33% improvement over heuristic methods on the BIRD benchmark.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Multilingual Polarization Detection Using Transformer-Based Models with Class Weighting and Threshold Tuning
item.description: Transformer-based models with class weighting and threshold tuning achieve competitive performance on multilingual polarization detection for English and Swahili.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Training Therapeutic Judges and Multi-Agent Systems for Human-Aligned Mental Health Support
item.description: Study introduces TheraJudge and TheraAgent, systems that evaluate and refine LLM-generated mental health responses using human-aligned metrics, improving therapeutic quality by 0.43 points.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Beyond Clean Text: Evaluating Encoder and Decoder Robustness for Bangla Event Detection in Noisy Text
item.description: Bangla event detection benchmark reveals encoder models outperform on clean text but degrade with noise, while decoder LLMs remain robust.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Bridging Scientific Heritage: An Arabic--Russian Parallel Corpus and LLM Benchmark for Sustainable Knowledge Transfer
item.description: Study releases 27,000-sentence Arabic-Russian scientific parallel corpus and benchmarks three fine-tuned multilingual language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Linguistic Distancing on Social Media: Indicators of Emotion Regulation Across Age Groups
item.description: Researchers analyze linguistic distancing on social media across age groups, finding increased distancing markers with age as a potential indicator of emotion regulation and well-being.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] From Propositional to Perceptual Asymmetry: Extending Frictive Policy Optimization to Asymmetric Partial Information Dialogue
item.description: Study extends dialogue optimization to handle perceptual asymmetry, showing informed single viewpoint outperforms omniscient access to all participants' information states.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Measuring Judgment Quality in Natural-Language Explanations: Evidence from Forecasting Tournaments
item.description: Researchers introduce Explanation Quality Markers (EQMs), using LLMs to score reasoning patterns in written explanations, showing they predict forecasting accuracy better than traditional methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Wait, am I Being Fair? Characterizing Deductive Stereotyping and Mitigating It with Fair-GCG
item.description: Researchers identify deductive stereotyping in LLMs and propose Fair-GCG, a framework using injection phrases to improve fairness-aware reasoning across models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CORTEX: Token-Level Hallucination Detection in RAG via Comparative Internal Representations
item.description: CORTEX enables token-level hallucination detection in RAG by comparing LLM representations with and without retrieved documents, reducing false positives.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Truth or Sophistry? LoFa: A Benchmark for LLM Robustness Against Logical Fallacies
item.description: LoFa benchmark measures LLM vulnerability to logical fallacies, revealing varying robustness across model types.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Semantic-Layer-Mediated Agent for Natural Language to SQL over Heterogeneous Enterprise Databases
item.description: Semantic-layer-mediated NL2SQL agent achieves 94.15% execution accuracy on enterprise database queries, ranking third on the Spider2-snow benchmark.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Reference-Based Prosody and Rhythm Evaluation for Spoken Dialogue Systems
item.description: Study proposes matched-reference evaluation protocol for prosody and rhythm in speech-to-speech AI using human conversation data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Exploring the relationship between team institutional composition and novelty in academic papers based on fine-grained knowledge entities
item.description: Academic-industrial collaboration produces more novel NLP papers than purely industrial teams, focusing on method-metric versus method-tool novelty.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Building a Multimodal Dataset of Academic Paper for Keyword Extraction
item.description: Researchers created a 1000-sample multimodal dataset for keyword extraction from academic papers, finding that combining text, image, and audio modalities improves extraction performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Triospect: A Three-Dimensional Framework for Robust Statistical AI-Generated Text Detection Against Diverse Attacks
item.description: Triospect framework improves AI-generated text detection by analyzing content and expression, achieving 22.3% AUROC gains against diverse attacks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Reranking Hurts: Uncertainty-Based Gating for Few-Shot Reranking
item.description: Training-free gated reranking uses model uncertainty to decide when to rerank few-shot examples, reducing costs 15-80% and improving performance up to 2%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] What Counts as an Error? Dual-Reference Benchmarking for Atypical ASR
item.description: Benchmarking ASR on atypical speech reveals model performance and rankings differ when using verbatim versus intended transcription references, highlighting reference selection importance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SeKV: Resolution-Adaptive KV Cache with Hierarchical Semantic Memory for Long-Context LLM Inference
item.description: SeKV introduces a hierarchical GPU-CPU KV cache system for LLMs that reduces memory by 53% while maintaining performance through adaptive token reconstruction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] TAG-DLM: Diffusion Language Models for Text-Attributed Graph Learning
item.description: Diffusion language models with topology attention masks unify text reasoning and graph structure learning for text-attributed graphs, outperforming existing methods without fine-tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Gated Multi-Graph Fusion via Graph Attention Networks for Alzheimer's Disease Detection
item.description: Graph attention network analyzes speech patterns across multiple linguistic views to detect Alzheimer's disease with 90% accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Can LLMs Imagine Moral Alternatives Beyond Binary Dilemmas?
item.description: LLMs can generate moral alternatives to binary dilemmas that often outperform original options and better satisfy ethical criteria.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Probing Stylistic Appropriation using Large Language Models: An Evaluation Framework for Copyright Infringement under EU Law
item.description: Research demonstrates LLMs fine-tuned on copyrighted works exhibit stylistic appropriation beyond memorization, exposing insufficient EU copyright safeguards.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When the Database Fails: Prompting LLM Dialogue Agents for Safe Recovery in Task-Oriented Dialogue
item.description: Prompting-based recovery reduces LLM hallucination in task-oriented dialogue by 42-50% when databases fail, without retraining models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LOPA: Enhancing Spoken Language Assessment via Latent Ordinal Prototype Alignment
item.description: LOPA achieves spoken language assessment performance rivaling billion-parameter models using ordinal prototype alignment, without LLM-based fine-tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Joint discovery of governing partial differential equations from multi-source datasets by competitive optimization
item.description: Framework discovers shared governing equations from multi-source datasets using competitive optimization and neural surrogates.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Accelerometry-Derived Digital Biomarkers for Cardiometabolic Risk: A Population-Representative Tabular Benchmark with Uncertainty Quantification
item.description: Benchmark evaluates ML methods for predicting cardiometabolic biomarkers from accelerometry, finding TabPFN v2 performs best but reveals demographic fairness gaps.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] From Search to Synthesis: Training LLMs as Zero-Shot Workflow Generators
item.description: MetaFlow trains LLMs via meta-learning to generate reusable workflows, using supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning with execution feedback.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Why Do Few-Step Text Latents Fail When Image Latents Work? Non-Commitment at Sharp Categorical Readouts
item.description: Decoder sharpness, not training deficiency, explains why few-step deterministic generation succeeds for images but fails for text.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Hierarchical Global Attention (HGA)
item.description: Hierarchical Global Attention reduces GPU memory for long-context transformers by routing tokens between host storage and GPU without retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ReactionAtlas: Ab origine exploration of chemical reaction networks with machine learning
item.description: ReactionAtlas uses machine learning to map chemical reaction networks ab origine, discovering approximately 47,000 reactions from eight pre-biotic seeds.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Revocable Learned State via Process Sidecars
item.description: Method revokes learned model memory from earlier training stages while accounting for transformations introduced by later safety optimization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Predictable GRPO: A Closed-Form Model of Training Dynamics
item.description: First-principles model explains Group Relative Policy Optimization training dynamics, predicting group-size invariance and providing failure-mode diagnostics.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Gradient Smoothing: Coupling Layer-wise Updates for Improved Optimization
item.description: Gradient Smoothing improves optimization by coupling layer-wise updates along depth dimension, enhancing performance across language models, diffusion models, and vision transformers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Mind the Residual Gap: Probabilistic Downscaling under Real-World Bias
item.description: ReMatch aligns training and test-time residual distributions using optimal transport to improve probabilistic downscaling calibration and reduce ensemble under-dispersion.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Partition-Guided Distance Saliency: Bridging Decision and Objective Spaces in Many-Objective Optimization
item.description: PGDS framework bridges decision and objective spaces in many-objective optimization via surrogate learning and automated feature sensitivity analysis.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Stationary-Distribution Theory for Triplet-Based Plateau Search in Random Forest Ensemble-Size Selection
item.description: Theory characterizes equilibrium behavior of plateau-based Random Forest ensemble-size selection as a stochastic birth-death process.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Transferable Learned Temporal Prior for Transmission Reconstruction and Decision-Relevant Uncertainty in Real Outbreak Labels
item.description: A pre-trained temporal prior for outbreak transmission outperforms source-specific models and reveals that transmission label uncertainty significantly affects public health prioritization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Behavior Cloning is Not All You Need: The Optimality of On-Policy Distillation for Noisy Expert Feedback
item.description: On-policy distillation provably outperforms offline behavior cloning when learning from noisy expert feedback, requiring polynomial versus exponential sample complexity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Personalizing Marketplace Policies with Competing Objectives and Constrained Experiments: Evidence from a Job Marketplace
item.description: Researchers develop a framework for personalizing free-value policies in job marketplaces, using ensemble models to balance competing employer and job-seeker objectives.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Quality-Aware Modulation for Diffusion Transformers
item.description: Researchers propose Quality Representation Module (QRM) for diffusion transformers to improve image quality by injecting quality-aware modulation signals into the denoising process.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Physics-informed Conditional Normalizing Flows for Angles-only Cislunar Orbit Determination
item.description: Physics-informed normalizing flows determine cislunar orbits from angle-only observations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Multistage Defer Trees for Hybrid Interpretability: If at First You Can't Succeed, Tree Again
item.description: Multistage Defer Trees sequence sparse decision trees for most samples while deferring difficult cases, balancing interpretability with ensemble-level accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Estimating Supply Incrementality in Two-sided Marketplaces: A Causal Machine Learning Approach
item.description: Researchers apply causal machine learning to estimate how additional supply affects two-sided marketplace outcomes, demonstrated on Airbnb booking data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Offline Reinforcement Learning for Fluid Controls: Data-based Multi-observational Policy Extraction
item.description: Data-driven offline RL framework extracts adaptive flow control policies that generalize across multiple sensor configurations without requiring expensive real-time environment interactions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] OTCache: Optimal Transport for Geometry-Aware Caching in Diffusion Models
item.description: OTCache accelerates diffusion sampling 4.5-4.7x by predicting caching schedules using Optimal Transport, while improving generation fidelity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Teaching LLMs to Recommend and Defer in Underrepresented Epilepsy Care
item.description: Researchers develop MANANA, a prompt-learning framework enabling LLMs to recommend anti-seizure medications while deferring uncertain cases in Ugandan pediatric epilepsy care.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Warp RL: Reshaping Base Policy Distributions for Dynamics Adaptation
item.description: Warp RL replaces additive policy corrections with invertible transformations for better robot adaptation under dynamics shifts.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Knowledge Distillation from Large Reasoning Models to Compact Student Models: A Case Study on the John O Bryan Mathematics Competition
item.description: Researchers distill DeepSeek-R1's reasoning into compact Qwen2.5-7B model, achieving 4.76 percentage-point accuracy gain on mathematics problems.
